user_query:
We had a brief outage last night and I want to tighten our OpenClaw gateway guardian settings based on actual behavior. Please do the following using only the files under input/ and write all results under output/:

1) Read input/health_log.json (an array of objects with fields including timestamp and status) and input/config_base.json (our current guardian config). Treat a record as a "check event" only when status is one of: "healthy", "port_closed", "process_missing", or "port_timeout". Ignore non-check events like "restart_attempted" and "restart_success" for check counts.

2) Create output/normalized_health.jsonl where you output exactly one JSON object per line for each check event in ascending timestamp order, with this shape and logic:
- {"ts": <timestamp string copied from the event>, "status": <original status>, "ok": true if status=="healthy" else false, "type": "check"}
Do not include any non-check events in this file.

3) Compute metrics strictly by these rules and write them to output/health_summary.json as a single JSON object with the following fields:
- total_checks: number of check events as defined above
- healthy_count: number of check events with status=="healthy"
- unhealthy_count: total_checks - healthy_count
- restart_attempts: count of events with status=="restart_attempted"
- restart_successes: count of events with status=="restart_success"
- last_check_status: the status of the last check event in timestamp order
- failure_sequences_of_3_plus: the number of maximal consecutive runs (in timestamp order) of non-healthy check events (i.e., status in {"port_closed","process_missing","port_timeout"}) whose length is >= 3. Runs are delimited by any healthy event.
- success_rate: round(healthy_count / total_checks, 3) using standard rounding to three decimal places (e.g., 0.833)
Also include a nested object recommended_config computed exactly as:
- port: copy gateway.port from input/config_base.json unchanged
- check_interval_seconds: set to 30
- max_restart_attempts: if restart_attempts > 5 then 12 else 10
- restart_delay_seconds: if failure_sequences_of_3_plus >= 2 then 7 else 5

4) Produce output/optimized_config.json by taking input/config_base.json and updating only these fields under the gateway object to the values from recommended_config above: check_interval_seconds, max_restart_attempts, restart_delay_seconds. Do not change gateway.port or any other keys or values anywhere else in the file (e.g., notifications, logging, tokens must be preserved exactly). If any of the three fields are missing in the base file, add them under gateway; otherwise, overwrite their values.

File paths: read input/health_log.json and input/config_base.json; write output/normalized_health.jsonl, output/health_summary.json, and output/optimized_config.json. Make sure all output JSON is syntactically valid.
